exact_answer: Cushing's disease is the most common cause of Cushing's syndrome, accounting for 70% of cases.

Cushing's syndrome results from sustained pathologic hypercortisolism caused by excessive corticotropin (ACTH) secretion by tumors in the pituitary gland (Cushing's disease, 70%) or elsewhere (15%), or by ACTH-independent cortisol secretion from adrenal tumors (15%).
Differentiating a pituitary source of excess ACTH (Cushing's disease)
Endogenous Cushing's syndrome is caused either by excess ACTH secretion or by autonomous cortisol release from the adrenal cortex.
